deep_dive.title: Last But Not Least: Boundary Attention CalibratiON for Multimodal KV Cache Compression
deep_dive.abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve strong vision-language reasoning, but long visual contexts enlarge the KV cache and increase decoding latency. Existing compression methods rely on observation window attention for stable token-importance estimation, yet this aggregation can dilute sparse visual evidence and discard answer-critical tokens under aggressive compression. Therefore, we identify last-query attention as a complementary source for recovering such evidence, but its answer-irrelevant signals can mislead retention. We propose BACON, a plug-and-play method that calibrates observation window attention with last-query evidence and suppresses isolated noise via intra-layer coherence and inter-layer persistence. Across diverse benchmarks, models, budgets, and compression methods, BACON improves multimodal KV compression by 7.5% on average under the most aggressive budget, with gains up to 30.9%. Our project page is available at https://ryu1ion.github.io/official_BACON/
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV cache compression for multimodal LLM inference acceleration
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 多模态LLM的KV缓存压缩以加速推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 多模态LLM的KV缓存压缩以加速推理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 多模态LLM的KV缓存压缩以加速推理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: BACON calibrates observation window and last-query attention to compress multimodal KV cache, reducing decoding latency without losing critical visual tokens.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态大语言模型面临长视觉上下文导致KV缓存大、解码延迟高的问题。现有压缩方法基于观察窗口注意力可能丢失关键视觉token。本文提出BACON，通过引入最后查询注意力信息并进行校准，同时利用层内一致性和层间持续性抑制噪声，从而有效压缩KV缓存并保持准确性。实验表明，BACON在多种多模态任务上显著降低延迟，同时保持甚至提升生成质量，为VLM高效推理提供了实用方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 多模态大语言模型面临长视觉上下文导致KV缓存大、解码延迟高的问题。现有压缩方法基于观察窗口注意力可能丢失关键视觉token。本文提出BACON，通过引入最后查询注意力信息并进行校准，同时利用层内一致性和层间持续性抑制噪声，从而有效压缩KV缓存并保持准确性。实验表明，BACON在多种多模态任务上显著降低延迟，同时保持甚至提升生成质量，为VLM高效推理提供了实用方案。

deep_dive.title: Express Language Modeling
deep_dive.abstract: We introduce a new tool, Express, for converting a non-causal attention approximation into a causal approximation with matching approximation guarantees. When combined with the state-of-the-art Thinformer approximation, Express improves upon the best known causal attention guarantees, delivering $\log^{3/2}(n)/s$ approximation error with only $O(s)$ memory and $O(s^2 \log^2(n))$ compression overhead for a sequence of length $n$. We pair these developments with an efficient I/O-aware Triton implementation, demonstrate substantial speedups over FlashAttention 2, and use Express to overcome four resource bottlenecks in the language modeling pipeline: long-context prefill, KV cache compression, long-form memory-constrained decoding, and long-form compute-constrained decoding.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Express tool for KV cache compression and long-context decoding acceleration, outperforming FlashAttention 2
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: Express工具实现KV缓存压缩和长上下文解码加速，性能优于FlashAttention 2
deep_dive.llm_evidence: Express工具实现KV缓存压缩和长上下文解码加速，性能优于FlashAttention 2
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: Express工具实现KV缓存压缩和长上下文解码加速，性能优于FlashAttention 2
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Express, a tool that converts non-causal attention approximations to causal with guarantees, enabling KV cache compression and speedups over FlashAttention 2 for long-context prefill, decoding, and memory-constrained generation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型长上下文推断中的KV缓存和计算瓶颈，提出Express工具，可将非因果注意力近似转换为因果近似并提供理论保证。结合Thinformer实现O(s)内存和亚线性近似误差，通过I/O高效的Triton实现显著超越FlashAttention 2。Express克服了长上下文预填充、KV缓存压缩、长序列解码和计算受限解码四个资源瓶颈，为大语言模型高效推理提供了通用解决方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对大语言模型长上下文推断中的KV缓存和计算瓶颈，提出Express工具，可将非因果注意力近似转换为因果近似并提供理论保证。结合Thinformer实现O(s)内存和亚线性近似误差，通过I/O高效的Triton实现显著超越FlashAttention 2。Express克服了长上下文预填充、KV缓存压缩、长序列解码和计算受限解码四个资源瓶颈，为大语言模型高效推理提供了通用解决方案。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 大语言模型高效推理相关的论文检索, especially work that connects or combines: efficient large language model inference techniques; KV cache optimization and management for LLM; speculative decoding for faster LLM inference; structured and unstructured pruning for LLMs; efficient attention mechanisms like FlashAttention or PagedAttention; survey on efficient inference of large language models; state of the art techniques for LLM inference acceleration; optimizing throughput and latency for LLM serving.

deep_dive.title: Last But Not Least: Boundary Attention CalibratiON for Multimodal KV Cache Compression
deep_dive.abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve strong vision-language reasoning, but long visual contexts enlarge the KV cache and increase decoding latency. Existing compression methods rely on observation window attention for stable token-importance estimation, yet this aggregation can dilute sparse visual evidence and discard answer-critical tokens under aggressive compression. Therefore, we identify last-query attention as a complementary source for recovering such evidence, but its answer-irrelevant signals can mislead retention. We propose BACON, a plug-and-play method that calibrates observation window attention with last-query evidence and suppresses isolated noise via intra-layer coherence and inter-layer persistence. Across diverse benchmarks, models, budgets, and compression methods, BACON improves multimodal KV compression by 7.5% on average under the most aggressive budget, with gains up to 30.9%.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes BACON for multimodal KV cache compression to reduce decoding latency in MLLMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出BACON方法用于多模态KV缓存压缩以减少MLLM解码延迟
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出BACON方法用于多模态KV缓存压缩以减少MLLM解码延迟

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: BACON calibrates observation window attention with last-query evidence to improve KV cache compression for multimodal LLMs, reducing latency without sacrificing answer-critical tokens.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对多模态大语言模型在长视觉上下文下KV缓存膨胀导致解码延迟增加的问题，提出了一种即插即用的注意力校准方法BACON。BACON通过引入最后查询注意力的互补证据，并利用层内一致性和层间持久性抑制孤立噪声，实现了更有效的KV缓存压缩。实验表明，该方法在保持答案关键信息的同时显著降低了解码延迟。该工作为多模态LLM的高效推理提供了新的压缩思路。

quick_skim.title: miniReranker: Efficient Multimodal Reranking through Visual Cache Reuse and Interaction Sparsity
quick_skim.abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have recently shown strong potential as point-wise rerankers by directly modeling query--document relevance through next-token prediction. However, point-wise reranking suffers from substantial repeated computation across query--document pairs, while the causal structure of transformers allows only prefix segments to be reused via pre-caching. To address the misalignment of existing query-first and document-first formats with both VQA-style prompting and computation-aware reuse, we propose a $\textit{vision-first}$ formulation that improves both cache reuse efficiency and reranking performance. However, the remaining cost is still considerable and stems from three main sources: (1) $\textit{model depth}$, for which we reduce active parameters via early exit; (2) $\textit{cross-segment attention}$, which we restrict to a narrow interaction band across a few layers; and (3) $\textit{visual tokens}$, where we reduce the number of tokens via embedder-guided pruning. Together, these designs form miniReranker, which reduces reranking runtime to <1% of the dense implementation under high-reuse settings for a single query, while preserving >96% of the dense model performance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: visual cache reuse and interaction sparsity for efficient multimodal reranking
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过视觉缓存重用和交互稀疏性实现高效多模态重排序
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过视觉缓存重用和交互稀疏性实现高效多模态重排序
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过视觉缓存重用和交互稀疏性实现高效多模态重排序
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: miniReranker proposes a vision-first format to improve cache reuse and reduce computation in multimodal point-wise reranking, achieving efficient inference.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态大模型作为点式重排序器时，计算重复严重。本文提出视觉优先格式，利用缓存重用和交互稀疏性大幅降低计算成本。实验表明在保持重排序质量的同时显著提升速度，为高效多模态排序提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多模态大模型作为点式重排序器时，计算重复严重。本文提出视觉优先格式，利用缓存重用和交互稀疏性大幅降低计算成本。实验表明在保持重排序质量的同时显著提升速度，为高效多模态排序提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Efficient On-Device Diffusion LLM Inference with Mobile NPU
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) accelerate generation by denoising multiple tokens in parallel, making them attractive for latency-sensitive mobile inference. However, repeated denoising introduces substantial computation on smartphones. Mobile neural processing units (NPUs) offer high-throughput dense matrix computation, but efficiently exploiting them remains challenging: token commitment shrinks per-block effective workloads, token revision complicates KV cache reuse, and limited NPU-visible address space incurs costly remapping and data transfer overheads.   In this paper, we propose llada.cpp, the first NPU-aware inference framework for accelerating dLLMs on smartphones. llada.cpp aligns block-wise dLLM inference with the execution characteristics of mobile NPUs through three techniques. (1) Multi-Block Speculative Decoding fills the shrinking workload in late-stage current-block decoding with speculative future-block tokens. (2) Dual-Path Progressive Revision keeps committed tokens revisable until stable and refreshes unstable tokens through a CPU-side path without stalling dense NPU execution. (3) Swap-Optimized Memory Runtime compacts NPU-visible address layouts and overlaps data staging with NPU computation to reduce remapping and transfer overheads. We implement llada.cpp as an end-to-end framework and evaluate it across diverse hardware platforms and dLLM workloads. llada.cpp reduces LLaDA-8B generation latency by 17x-42x over the CPU baseline with prefix KV cache reuse, while preserving generation quality.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes llada.cpp, an NPU-aware inference framework for accelerating diffusion LLMs on smartphones
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出llada.cpp，一种面向移动NPU的扩散LLM推理加速框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出llada.cpp，一种面向移动NPU的扩散LLM推理加速框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出llada.cpp，一种面向移动NPU的扩散LLM推理加速框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: llada.cpp aligns block-wise diffusion LLM inference with NPU characteristics, accelerating generation on smartphones.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对扩散大语言模型在手机上推理时计算量大、NPU利用率低的问题，提出了llada.cpp框架。该框架通过将块级扩散LLM推理与NPU执行特点对齐，解决了令牌承诺导致的负载缩减、令牌修订引起的KV缓存重用困难以及地址空间限制带来的数据拷贝开销。实验表明，llada.cpp在移动设备上显著加速了扩散LLM推理，为端侧大模型高效部署提供了新方案。

quick_skim.title: Task-Aware Structured Memory for Dynamic Multi-modal In-Context Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) depend on in-context learning (ICL) for rapid task adaptation, but their scalability is severely limited by finite context windows and the growing cost of key-value (KV) caches in long multi-modal sequences. Existing memory compression approaches typically rely on rigid token removal or sample-dependent importance estimation, which introduces bias, disrupts semantic structure, particularly for visual representations, and yields static memories that cannot adapt to new queries. We introduce TASM (Task-Aware Structured Memory), a training-free framework that addresses these limitations through task-aware, structure-preserving, and dynamically accessible memory construction. TASM employs task-vector guided compression to replace sample-specific signals with a task-level direction that captures shared relevance across demonstrations. To preserve the underlying manifold, it applies semantics-aware token merging via bipartite graph matching, aggregating tokens without destructive pruning. Finally, TASM structures memory into a hierarchy comprising a compact Core Memory and a Latent Bank, facilitating query-adaptive dynamic retrieval. Evaluations confirm TASM maintains high performance under heavy compression, effectively balancing efficiency with adaptability.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: KV cache optimization for multi-modal LLM inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向多模态大模型推理的KV缓存优化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向多模态大模型推理的KV缓存优化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向多模态大模型推理的KV缓存优化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes TASM, a training-free framework that compresses KV cache for multi-modal in-context learning via task-vector guidance, alleviating context length limitations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多模态大语言模型在长序列上下文学习时KV缓存膨胀的问题，提出无训练的任务感知结构记忆（TASM）框架。TASM利用任务向量引导压缩，保留语义结构并动态适应新查询，有效减少缓存占用，提升多模态推理效率。实验表明在多种多模态任务上保持或提升了性能，为高效多模态大模型推理提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Mean-Field Parallel Decoding for Discrete Diffusion Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Discrete diffusion language models enable parallel token generation, offering a pathway to low-latency decoding. However, selecting tokens independently by marginal confidence limits effective parallelism: tokens that appear reliable in isolation can form incompatible configurations when several positions are updated at once. We introduce a training-free decoding framework that coordinates these parallel updates. At each forward pass, the method assigns a commit score to each masked position and refines these scores using pairwise interactions derived from the model's predictive distributions. A variational relaxation yields a simple fixed-point update that suppresses conflicting simultaneous commitments within a single forward pass. This mechanism allows the decoder to commit more tokens in parallel while maintaining competitive generation quality. The method is lightweight, requires no auxiliary model or retraining, and drops into existing diffusion decoding pipelines without modification. Experiments on reasoning and code-generation benchmarks show consistent improvements in the quality-latency trade-off.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Parallel decoding framework for low-latency language model inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于低延迟语言模型推断的并行解码框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于低延迟语言模型推断的并行解码框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于低延迟语言模型推断的并行解码框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: A training-free mean-field parallel decoding framework for discrete diffusion LMs that coordinates parallel token updates via commit scores and pairwise interactions to avoid incompatibility, enabling faster generation with maintained quality.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对离散扩散语言模型并行解码中独立选择标记导致不兼容配置的问题，提出了一种训练无关的均场并行解码框架。该框架通过分配承诺分数并利用模型预测分布的成对交互进行细化，在单次前向传播中抑制冲突更新。实验表明该方法能实现更快的并行生成同时保持生成质量，为扩散语言模型的高效推理提供了新途径。

quick_skim.title: Acting While Understanding: Asynchronous Semantic-Action Decoupling for Real-Time Vision-Language-Action Models
quick_skim.abstract: Vision-Language-Action models (VLAs) have demonstrated strong task understanding and generalization in robotic manipulation, yet the high computational cost of full-model inference limits their deployment in low-latency, high-frequency closed-loop control. We propose an asynchronous semantic-action decoupling framework that separates semantic understanding from action generation along the internal semantic-action interface of existing VLAs, without redesigning the vision-language backbone or introducing an external planner. A low-frequency understanding module asynchronously updates reusable semantic conditions, while a high-frequency action module continuously outputs control actions without repeatedly invoking the full model. To mitigate the temporal mismatch between stale semantics and the current execution state, we further introduce historical action conditioning and time-misalignment training, which provide short-horizon execution context and improve feedback control robustness under stale semantic conditions. Experiments on LIBERO with $π_{0.5}$ and UniVLA, together with real-robot deployment using UniVLA, show that the proposed framework achieves up to 35.6 Hz server-side action-module inference throughput and offers a low-intrusion path to high-frequency closed-loop control without running full VLA inference at control rate.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: asynchronous semantic-action decoupling for low-latency VLA model inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 异步语义-动作解耦以实现低延迟视觉-语言-动作模型推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 异步语义-动作解耦以实现低延迟视觉-语言-动作模型推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 异步语义-动作解耦以实现低延迟视觉-语言-动作模型推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Asynchronous semantic-action decoupling separates understanding and action generation in VLAs, enabling high-frequency control without full-model reinvocation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 视觉-语言-动作模型（VLA）推理成本高，难以满足实时控制需求。本文提出异步语义-动作解耦框架，沿VLA内部接口分离语义理解与动作生成：低频模块异步更新语义条件，高频模块持续输出动作。该方法无需重新设计主干网络或引入外部规划器，在机器人操作任务中显著降低了推理延迟，实现了高频率的闭环控制。该工作为VLA模型的实时部署提供了有效方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 视觉-语言-动作模型（VLA）推理成本高，难以满足实时控制需求。本文提出异步语义-动作解耦框架，沿VLA内部接口分离语义理解与动作生成：低频模块异步更新语义条件，高频模块持续输出动作。该方法无需重新设计主干网络或引入外部规划器，在机器人操作任务中显著降低了推理延迟，实现了高频率的闭环控制。该工作为VLA模型的实时部署提供了有效方案。

quick_skim.title: Follow the Latent Roadmap: Navigating Revocable Decoding for Diffusion LLMs with Anchor Tokens
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion Large Language Models (dLLMs) offer a promising avenue for parallel generation but face a trade-off between decoding speed and quality. While revocable decoding strategies attempt to mitigate errors by verifying and remasking tokens, they typically operate within a mixed-quality context. This leads to two critical failures: \textit{Error Propagation}, where new tokens absorb toxic information from erroneous context, and \textit{Local Error Reinforcement}, where errors mutually reinforce each other to evade detection. To alleviate these challenges, we propose ASRD (Anchor Supervised Revocable Decoding), a training-free framework that operates within the embedding space. ASRD explicitly decouples the decoding context into trusted \textit{Anchor Tokens}, which are identified via temporal consistency, and uncertain candidates. Leveraging a dynamic Anchor Tokens Cache, we introduce two complementary mechanisms: (1) Anchor-Guided Generation, which injects entropy-weighted anchor signals into masked positions to implicitly rectify attention toward the reliable global skeleton; and (2) Anchor-Perturbed Verification, which applies orthogonal perturbations to uncertain candidate tokens, destabilizing and remasking errors driven by fragile local consensus. Extensive experiments on math and coding benchmarks demonstrate that ASRD outperforms recent remasking baselines, achieving accuracy improvements of up to 6.4\% while accelerating inference throughput by up to 7.2$\times$.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: verification-based decoding strategy for language models, similar to speculative decoding verification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于验证的解码策略，类似投机解码中的验证机制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于验证的解码策略，类似投机解码中的验证机制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于验证的解码策略，类似投机解码中的验证机制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes ASRD, a training-free framework that uses anchor tokens to decouple decoding context in diffusion LLMs, enabling effective error detection and remasking in revocable decoding.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对扩散大语言模型可撤销解码中的错误传播与局部错误强化问题，提出无训练的锚点监督可撤销解码（ASRD）框架。ASRD通过锚点令牌在嵌入空间中解耦上下文，显式分离可信与错误信息，从而提升错误检测与修正能力。实验表明该方法有效改善了并行解码的质量与可靠性，为语言模型的高效验证解码提供了新视角，可启发投机解码中的验证策略设计。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对扩散大语言模型可撤销解码中的错误传播与局部错误强化问题，提出无训练的锚点监督可撤销解码（ASRD）框架。ASRD通过锚点令牌在嵌入空间中解耦上下文，显式分离可信与错误信息，从而提升错误检测与修正能力。实验表明该方法有效改善了并行解码的质量与可靠性，为语言模型的高效验证解码提供了新视角，可启发投机解码中的验证策略设计。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 基于宽松验证的大预言模型投机解码方法, especially work that connects or combines: speculative decoding for large language models; relaxed verification mechanism in speculative decoding; approximate matching for speculative execution; relaxed acceptance criteria for token generation; lossy verification in LLM inference acceleration; accelerating LLM inference with speculative decoding; speculative decoding methods with relaxed verification for LLMs; approximate token matching in speculative decoding frameworks; improving speculative decoding efficiency via relaxed acceptance.
